title: daucus carota subsp. gummifer
intro: Daucus carota subsp. gummifer, also known as wild carrot or gummiferous carrot, is a subspecies of wild carrot (Daucus carota) that grows in the wild and is characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and act as a natural diuretic. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. gummifer include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
